qa.overall_assessment: BA spec has one critical accuracy defect: the framing premise that both fixes are 'in the working tree but uncommitted' is factually wrong — the fixes were already committed in becffc6 before this spec was authored. All four in-scope files already contain the correct working-tree code at HEAD. The wildcard predicate (allowlist.py:521-523), candidate-list (pretool-write-guard.sh:59-65), and mirrored posttool candidate-list (posttool-allowlist-consume.py:148-155) are all committed and present. The test gap (no test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path) is correctly identified and confirmed as real. The fix prescription (add one test, commit four files) is correct in substance — but 'verify and commit' is misleading for M1/M2/M3 which are already committed. This causes a spec_text_vs_execution_drift: BA's AC-level verification recipe remains correct (pytest / bash -n / py_compile all work), but the 'codebase_state' claim in context JSON and the 'working tree has uncommitted fixes' framing must be corrected so dev does not waste time looking for uncommitted diffs that do not exist.

objections.finding: BA's core framing claim is factually wrong: 'Working tree already contains uncommitted fixes for both bugs' (context JSON codebase_state field) and 'Both fixes exist in working tree but are uncommitted' (spec Goal section). In reality, the working tree is CLEAN — git status shows nothing to commit. The fixes ARE present in the code, but they were committed in becffc6 ('chore(dot-claude): ship cycles 20260521-20260524 hooks, commands, agents, docs and tests'), not left as working-tree changes.

evidence.git_status_output: nothing to commit, working tree clean
evidence.wildcard_predicate_confirmed_at_HEAD: hooks/lib/allowlist.py:521-523 — entry_target = entry.get('target'); if entry_target is None or entry_target == target_path: return entry
evidence.candidate_list_confirmed_at_HEAD: hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh:59-65 — writer_key = env_task_id if env_task_id else session_id; for k in [writer_key, data_task_id, session_id]

objections.impact: Dev will open the repo expecting uncommitted diffs and find none. The instruction 'verify and commit' for M1/M2/M3 is misleading — those three files are already committed. Dev must understand the actual task is: (1) add the missing test to test_allowlist_consolidation.py (the only genuinely incomplete item), (2) run tests to confirm 41+, (3) commit test_allowlist_consolidation.py only (not the three already-committed files).
objections.proposed_fix: Update context JSON codebase_state to: 'Working tree is CLEAN. Fixes for both bugs are already committed in becffc6. The only outstanding work is adding test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path to hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py.' Update spec Goal/Context framing to match. M1/M2/M3 requirements should read 'verify (already committed)' not 'verify and commit.'

objections.finding: BA cites 'Working-tree test suite: 40/40 pass; NO test for targetless wildcard case' and 'no test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path in TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite' — both claims verified correct against actual code. Test count confirmed: pytest collects 40 tests, 40 pass. grep for test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path returns no matches. However, BA also claims becffc6 committed 35 tests (aea75d9 had 35, becffc6 brought it to 40) by adding TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite with 5 cases. The 'NO test for targetless wildcard case' observation is correct but BA does not mention that the 5-case TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite class was itself shipped in becffc6 — this is consistent but the framing implies it was part of the uncommitted changes.

evidence.class_added_in_becffc6: TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite (5 tests: test_match_returns_entry, test_session_mismatch_returns_none, test_target_mismatch_returns_none, test_expired_sentinel_returns_none, test_op_mismatch_returns_none)
evidence.missing_test_confirmed: test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path absent — grep returns no output

objections.impact: Minor framing issue only. The test gap claim is accurate; the fix prescription (add one test) is correct.
objections.proposed_fix: Clarify that becffc6 committed the 5-test TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite class alongside the bug fixes; the only remaining gap is the 6th test for the wildcard case.

objections.finding: Both QA failures from prior cycle are fully addressed in the BA spec. Bug 1 (task_id key mismatch): the candidate-list fix at pretool-write-guard.sh:59-65 and posttool-allowlist-consume.py:148-155 directly resolves the session_id vs data.task_id mismatch. Bug 2 (targetless grant CF-1 regression): the wildcard predicate at allowlist.py:521-523 directly resolves the None == target_path false-match. Both root causes traced to the same commit (becffc6 / prior cycle). Scope is correctly bounded: userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh correctly excluded as belonging to sibling task 20260521-090200.

evidence.sibling_task_exclusion: userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh has diff vs aea75d9 (63 lines changed) — confirmed as sibling-task changes not included in this scope

objections.impact: No scope gap. Both QA-identified bugs addressed.

objections.finding: BA identified one missing test (test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path). BA also noted a Should Have: test_candidate_list_resolves_session_key (S1) which tests Bug 1 in integration. BA correctly flagged this as Should Have (not Must Have), with the observation that the working-tree live subprocess test confirmed the candidate-list works. However, BA did NOT investigate whether any test covers the posttool-allowlist-consume.py candidate-list path for Write sentinel consumption — the posttool uses match_sentinel_grant_for_write() internally, but there is no test in TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite that verifies the posttool's own candidate-list traversal. This is a gap in investigation completeness.

evidence.posttool_test_coverage_gap: No test in test_allowlist_consolidation.py exercises posttool-allowlist-consume.py Write elif branch with mismatched task_id and session_id keyed sentinel
evidence.grep_result: TestConsumeGrantForPosttool has no test for Write-sentinel consumption path — only Bash grant consumption is tested

objections.impact: Minor: the posttool Write path is structurally symmetric to the write-guard path and uses the same match_sentinel_grant_for_write() function, so the risk of an independent defect is low. The gap is a completeness miss, not a critical coverage hole.
objections.proposed_fix: Consider adding investigation note about posttool Write-consumption test coverage to out_of_scope_observations, or elevating to a third missing-test item. Not a blocker for the spec.

objections.finding: All four in-scope files exist and contain the code BA attributes to them. File paths verified: hooks/lib/allowlist.py (597 lines), hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh (128 lines), hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py (195 lines), hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py (636 lines). Line ranges cited by BA are accurate: allowlist.py:484-524 (match_sentinel_grant_for_write), pretool-write-guard.sh:46-96 (sentinel block with candidate-list at 59-65), posttool-allowlist-consume.py:140-190 (Write elif branch), test_allowlist_consolidation.py:534-633 (TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite). userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:183 correctly reads TASK_ID='${CLAUDE_TASK_ID:-${SID}}'. Exclusion of userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh from in-scope files is correct.

evidence.allowlist_py_wildcard_confirmed: lines 521-523: entry_target = entry.get('target'); if entry_target is None or entry_target == target_path: return entry
evidence.write_guard_candidate_list_confirmed: lines 59-65: writer_key = env_task_id if env_task_id else session_id; seen = set(); candidates = []; for k in [writer_key, data_task_id, session_id]
evidence.posttool_candidate_list_confirmed: lines 148-155: env_task_id = os.environ.get('CLAUDE_TASK_ID', ''); writer_primary = env_task_id if env_task_id else session_id; same priority order

objections.finding: AC verification recipes are runnable as-written. AC3: 'python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py -v' — confirmed runnable, currently 40 tests collected, 40 pass, 0 fail. AC4: 'bash -n hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh && python3 -m py_compile hooks/lib/allowlist.py hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py' — confirmed runnable, all exit 0. AC1 and AC2 are code-inspection / unit-test ACs that can be satisfied by the new test + pytest run. No spec_text_vs_execution_drift found in the AC recipes. The drift issue is in the narrative framing (uncommitted vs committed), not in the verification recipes.

verdict_detail.verdict_reason: OBJ-1 is blocking: BA's primary framing claim ('fixes are uncommitted in working tree') is demonstrably false. The working tree is clean. This will cause dev to search for uncommitted diffs that do not exist and misunderstand the scope of work. The spec must be corrected before dev proceeds: only test_allowlist_consolidation.py needs to be modified (add the one missing test); the other three files are already correct and committed.
verdict_detail.ac_deviation_detail: BA's M1/M2/M3 requirements say 'dev must verify and commit' — but the code is already committed. Only M4 (add test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path) represents genuinely incomplete work. The actual dev scope is narrower than the spec implies.
verdict_detail.substance_detail: Despite the framing error, BA correctly identifies: (1) the wildcard predicate fix is correct and present, (2) the candidate-list fix is correct and present, (3) the one missing test is correctly identified and described, (4) scope boundary (exclude userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh) is correct, (5) AC verification recipes are correct and runnable.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: task_id key mismatch: sentinel writer writes keyed by session_id ... sentinel reader looks up by data.task_id
success_criteria_results.criterion: write-guard resolves sentinel key via candidate-list [writer_key, data_task_id, session_id]; finds sentinel when CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset and sentinel keyed by session_id
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read pretool-write-guard.sh:59-65 and posttool-allowlist-consume.py:148-155 at HEAD
success_criteria_results.details: Candidate-list correctly implemented and committed in becffc6. Logic verified: writer_key = env_task_id if env_task_id else session_id; candidates = deduped [writer_key, data_task_id, session_id].

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: grants with no 'target' field (targetless, meaning 'allow any write') were previously allowed. After the Task B implementation, these targetless grants now trigger CF-1 denial
success_criteria_results.criterion: match_sentinel_grant_for_write returns entry (not None) when op=='Write' and target field is absent; new test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path passes
success_criteria_results.details: Wildcard predicate (entry_target is None or entry_target == target_path) is correctly committed at allowlist.py:521-523. BUT test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path is absent — grep confirms no match in test file. The fix is present; the test proving it is not.
success_criteria_results.evidence: allowlist.py:521-523 confirmed; grep test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path returns no output

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Both fixes must be backward-compatible and pass the existing test suite
success_criteria_results.criterion: All 40 pre-existing tests pass; no new regressions; syntax validation passes for all modified files
success_criteria_results.details: 40/40 tests pass. bash -n exits 0. py_compile exits 0 for all three Python files. No regressions detected.
success_criteria_results.evidence: pytest output: 40 passed in 0.04s; bash -n output: SYNTAX_OK; py_compile output: COMPILE_OK

all_findings.issue: BA claims 'Working tree already contains uncommitted fixes for both bugs' — but git status shows 'nothing to commit, working tree clean'. The fixes were committed in becffc6 before this spec was authored. Dev will search for uncommitted diffs that do not exist.
all_findings.recommendation: Correct codebase_state to reflect that fixes are already committed. Revise M1/M2/M3 to 'verify (already committed, no action needed)'. Only M4 requires dev action.

all_findings.issue: BA did not investigate posttool-allowlist-consume.py Write-consumption test coverage. No test in TestConsumeGrantForPosttool exercises the Write sentinel path with mismatched task_id, leaving the posttool's own candidate-list traversal untested at the integration level.
all_findings.recommendation: Note as investigation gap in out_of_scope_observations. Not a blocker.

failures.issue: BA spec framing error: 'fixes are in working tree, uncommitted' is factually incorrect. Working tree is clean. Fixes committed in becffc6. Dev action scope is only: add test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path and commit test_allowlist_consolidation.py — NOT 'verify and commit' all four files.
failures.recommendation: Reissue BA spec with corrected codebase_state and revised M1/M2/M3 wording before dev proceeds.

Test suite: 40/40 pass, 0 fail. test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path: ABSENT (grep confirms). Syntax: bash -n PASS, py_compile PASS for all three Python files.

The fixes for both Bug 1 and Bug 2 were committed in becffc6 before this spec was authored. The spec's M1/M2/M3 requirements say 'dev must verify and commit' but the files are already committed. Dev has no uncommitted diffs to commit for those three files. Only M4 (test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path) represents genuinely incomplete work.
spec_section_updates.section_7: Correct the BA spec before dispatching dev: (1) update context JSON codebase_state to 'working tree is clean; fixes committed in becffc6'; (2) revise M1/M2/M3 to 'already committed, verify only'; (3) update Development Approach to reflect that dev's only required modification is adding test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path to hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py; (4) update success_criteria[4] ('git diff HEAD for this task shows ONLY the four in-scope files') to reflect that only test_allowlist_consolidation.py will appear in the diff.

refined_context.recommended_approach: Reissue BA spec with: (1) codebase_state = 'working tree clean, fixes committed in becffc6'; (2) M1/M2/M3 = 'verify only (already committed)'; (3) M4 = 'add test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path (still required)'; (4) git diff success criterion updated to expect only test_allowlist_consolidation.py in diff.
